refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load→travel→dump cycle.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR included the phrase 'shall support achieving,' which can imply a solution approach. Replacing 'support achieving' with 'enable achieving' keeps the focus on the functional goal without implying how it is done. The FR is atomic as it focuses on a single functional objective: meeting the cycle time target for the specified cycle.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the cycle time for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, and verify that the 95th percentile (p95) cycle time is less than or equal to the customer-defined target (CT_target).
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase of the cycle.
The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the travel phase of the cycle.
The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the dump phase of the cycle.

candidate_dps: Optimize load handling mechanism to minimize load time
Optimize travel path and speed to minimize travel time
Optimize dump mechanism to minimize dump time
Coordinate load, travel, and dump operations to minimize total cycle time
Implement adaptive cycle time control based on real-time conditions
selected_dp.selected_dp: Coordinate load, travel, and dump operations to minimize total cycle time

potential_sources: Interdependence between load, travel, and dump operations timing
coupling_check.notes: Selecting coordination of all cycle phases as a single DP reduces risk of suboptimal independent optimization and supports holistic cycle time minimization.

assumptions_risks: Assumes accurate measurement and control of each cycle phase time
Risk that operational policies or safety plans may limit achievable cycle time improvements
Assumes site conditions remain within expected parameters during operation
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider refining the DP to explicitly include real-time monitoring and adaptive control to handle variability in site conditions and operational constraints.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Coordinates load, travel, and dump operations across vehicle and teleoperation to minimize total cycle time

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Exchange status and position data with Vehicle and Teleoperation

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receive high-level task commands for load, travel, and dump sequences

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receive safety alerts from Safety Services to adjust operations

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicate commands and status across distributed modules

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as it centrally manages coordination logic; low diffusion since it interfaces with other modules via defined interfaces.
Contains some operational logic but coordination across load, travel, and dump spans multiple modules, causing diffusion.
Focuses on remote control, not full coordination; would cause high diffusion if containing DP.
Primarily handles safety alerts, not operational coordination; poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the coordination DP because it integrates multiple operational aspects and manages inter-module communication, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential complexity within NGHE Solution may grow as coordination logic expands, risking reduced modular clarity.
Dependence on timely and reliable interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, Network Link) may introduce coupling risks.
If operational details are embedded too deeply in NGHE Solution, it may become a bottleneck for changes or scalability.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment risks grow, consider decomposing coordination into submodules within NGHE Solution to maintain modularity without spreading DP across unrelated modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by specifying coordination of load, travel, and dump operations to minimize total cycle time, directly targeting the customer-defined cycle time target. This mapping is clear and focused on achieving the required performance metric (p95 ≤ CT_target).

diffusion_risks: Potential complexity within NGHE Solution may grow as coordination logic expands, risking reduced modular clarity.
Dependence on timely and reliable interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, Network Link) may introduce coupling risks.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and low diffusion scores, indicating effective containment of the coordination DP. Other modules have lower containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the ownership boundary. However, risks exist if the module becomes too complex or tightly coupled to interfaces.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible by measuring the cycle time (p95) under agreed duty cycle and site conditions, and ensuring compliance with worksite safety plans and operational policies, which are supported by the interfaces and module responsibilities.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation is clear as the NGHE Solution module explicitly manages coordination across vehicle and teleoperation, interfacing with safety and communication modules, aligning well with the FR and DP.

recommended_revisions: Monitor the complexity growth within NGHE Solution and consider decomposing coordination logic into submodules to maintain modular clarity and scalability.
Ensure robust interface design and error handling for Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link to mitigate coupling risks.
Regularly review containment and diffusion metrics as the system evolves to prevent diffusion of the coordination DP across multiple modules.

decision.justification: The current FR to DP to IM mapping passes all gates with clear logical alignment, effective containment, and feasible verification. However, the complexity and coupling risks identified in the NGHE Solution module suggest that further decomposition of the coordination logic into sub-FRs is necessary to maintain modular clarity, scalability, and safety criticality. Decomposing will help isolate risks, improve interface robustness, and support ongoing containment monitoring.

revise.why: To address potential complexity growth and coupling risks within the NGHE Solution module by decomposing coordination logic into submodules, improving modular clarity and scalability.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module currently encapsulates all coordination logic for load, travel, and dump operations.
Interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are critical for coordination and require robust handling.
Safety criticality demands clear modular boundaries and manageable complexity.
open_risks: Complexity growth within NGHE Solution may reduce modular clarity and increase maintenance difficulty.
Coupling risks due to interface dependencies may impact reliability and safety.
Potential diffusion of coordination DP across multiple modules if not properly contained.
evidence_needed: Metrics on complexity and diffusion scores over time within NGHE Solution.
Verification results of cycle time (p95) under operational conditions.
Interface reliability and error handling performance data.
